# Awesome Claude Quant Scripts

> A collection of quant prompts and code that analyzes US equity and crypto markets by cross-validating one identical prompt across multiple LLMs (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, DeepSeek). The core methodology is Multi-LLM Cross-Validation: a single LLM cannot be the sole basis of trust, the consensus/divergence between models is itself a trading signal, humans apply the final cap on quantitative decisions, and each model exhibits a distinct investment "character" (Claude = disciplined/audit-ready, DeepSeek = aggressive alpha, ChatGPT = fast outlines, Gemini = compact/contrarian). Part of the vibe-investing series by Dennis Kim (HoKwang Kim). MIT License. For research and education only, not investment advice.

## Index

- [README (Korean)](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/blob/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Readme.MD): Korean entry point; explains the Multi-LLM Cross-Validation philosophy, all strategies sorted newest-first, and industry/type groupings.
- [README (English)](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/blob/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Readme%20EN.md): English version of the entry point.
- [Parent folder README](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/blob/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/README.md): The 01.Trading Strategy folder overview.
- [Main repo README](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/blob/main/README.md): Top-level vibe-investing repository overview and disclaimer.

## Strategies (newest first)

- [Midterm Signal](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Midterm%20signal) (2026-05-24, Macro/Timing): An LLM prompt plus self-run backtest that fact-checks the "buy the dip in a US midterm-election year" adage. Backtested 22 elections (1938-2022); shows the common "+36% one year later" claim assumes buying the exact bottom while the realistic expectation is ~+19%, the 95.5% win rate breaks in structural crises like 1939, and the effect is statistically insignificant. As of May 2026 it judges a "do not buy (2/6)" zone.
- [AI Super Cycle Weekly Rebalance](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Ai%20super%20cycle%20weekly%20rebalance%20prompt) (2026-05-21, AI/High-risk, 3-LLM): HIGH-RISK ultra-concentrated active strategy placing 25-44% in a single ticker (NVDA), dynamically adjusted by the 3-year GPU-demand cycle. A 3-LLM (Claude/ChatGPT/DeepSeek) cross-validation report: phase call was unanimous (ACCEL) but NVDA target weight diverged 35%-44%. Likely earns less than holding NVDA long-term; value is downside defense, not higher returns. Recommendation: 3-LLM median plus a human conservative cap.
- [Pharma Sector Long/Short](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Pharma%20sector%20prompt) (2026-05-04, Sector/Pharma-Bio, 4-LLM): A 12-24 month long/short portfolio across US pharma mega-caps. Four models produced four different portfolios; on more than half the mega-caps (BMY, AMGN, GILD, NVO, MRK) positions flipped between models, proving single-model reliance is a "per-model roulette." Demonstrates web-search usage determines data accuracy.
- [AI Supercycle Investment](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/AI%20Supercycle%20Investment%20Quant%20Strategy) (2026-05-02, AI/Tech): A complete bundle of 3-language (KR/EN/CN) LLM prompts, 3 recommended-portfolio CSVs, and a yfinance Python screener. Diversifies the AI value chain into 4 layers (Foundation semis, Infra hyperscalers, Enablers power/networking, Application) to recommend 10 tickers. Scoring: AI revenue exposure 35%, capital efficiency/momentum 30%, GARP valuation 20%, momentum/flows 15%.
- [Dividend Growth Prompt](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Dividend%20growth%20prompt) (2026-05-02, Dividend, 3-LLM): Discovers S&P 500 + NASDAQ-100 dividend-growth names and serves as an empirical case that "AI makes mistakes too." Compares Claude/Gemini/DeepSeek; only 2 tickers (MSFT, UNH) agreed by all three. Explicitly corrects each model's errors (Gemini sector misclassifications, DeepSeek sector violation and "too good to be true" Sharpe 1.45, Gemini score inflation).
- [Dual-Engine Capital Compounder](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Dual-Engine%20Capital%20Compounder%20Quant%20Strategy%20Using%20LLM) (2026-05-02, Dividend/Capital-return): A 2-engine system discovering US Financial (cards/banks/asset mgmt) and Retail dividend stocks. Core signal is TCRY (Total Capital Return Yield), adding buybacks to dividend yield. Financial Engine 5 (V, JPM, BLK, AXP, CB) + Retail Engine 5 (HD, TJX, WMT, MCD, TGT). 3-language prompts + CSV + Python screener.
- [AI Supply Chain Bayesian Analysis](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/AI%20supply%20chain%20bayesian%20analysis) (2026-04-27, Research/Quant): An LLM-based Bayesian multi-factor prompt analyzing the short-term (5-20 day) upside probability of AI supply-chain stocks. Sets macro context (rates, VIX, AI Capex) as prior, updates posterior via likelihood ratio. 5 layers x 3 factors (technical 30%, fundamental/macro 30%, flows/sentiment/macro 40%). Emphasizes reproducibility; scores are relative rankings, not absolute values.
- [DAT Quant Strategy](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/DAT%20quant%20strategy) (2026-04-27, Trading/Mean-reversion, Python+Kotlin): A 90-day rolling Z-Score pair-trading signal between DAT (Digital Asset Treasury) firms and their held crypto, across 7 pairs (MSTR, MARA, RIOT, GLXY, COIN, BMNR, SBET). Dual Python + Kotlin implementation. WARNING: if you want BTC exposure, buying Bitcoin directly is better than this; DAT firms add dilution/debt/management risk and the premium pattern can void on regime change (Spot BTC ETF, GENIUS Act).
- [Long-Term Dividend Investing](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Long-Term%20Dividend%20Investing) (2026-04-27, Dividend): A stock-selection script for long-term dividend investing, evaluating dividend growth, sustainability, payout ratio, and financial health. Code-implementation companion to the Dividend Growth Prompt; for retirement funding, income portfolios, and Dividend Aristocrats candidate discovery.
- [Declining Stock Quant Script Using LLM](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Declining%20Stock%20Quant%20Script%20Using%20LLM) (2025-04-26, Short/Bearish): An LLM prompt selecting S&P 500 / NASDAQ-100 constituents likely to decline within 1-4 weeks, plus matching inverse (short) positions. Multi-factor: technical 40%, fundamental 30%, flows/sentiment 30%. Explicitly warns about inverse-ETF volatility decay.
- [REGIME](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/REGIME) (Work-in-progress, Macro/Research): The "honest quant" prompt + Python pipeline analyzing macro variables (FX, rates, inflation, sentiment, oil) vs M7 + AI-infra stocks. Key finding: macro barely explains stocks (weekly regression R^2 of 1-3%); regimes inform timing, not stock selection; stripping QQQ beta nearly eliminates regime-dependence (significant names 3/17 to 1/17). Three approaches A/B/C. NOTE: incomplete; do not use without financial-engineering knowledge.
- [Sample](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/tree/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/Awesome%20claude%20quant%20scripts/Sample): A template folder with the standard structure (README, scripts, data, backtest, limitations) for starting a new strategy.

## Latest Research

- [ARDS - Adaptive Recession-Defensive Strategy](https://github.com/gameworkerkim/vibe-investing/blob/main/01.Trading%20Strategy/ARDS%3A%20Adaptive%20Recession-Defensive%20Strategy/readme.md) (2026-05-18, Defensive/Academic, 4-LLM): The definitive 4-LLM cross-validation study and the methodological endpoint of this collection. A dynamic defensive portfolio pursuing asymmetric alpha at recession onset, designed as the symmetric hedge to AMQS-M7. Tests the hypothesis that the future quant system is a committee of AIs with different philosophies, not a single super-intelligence. Findings: macro diagnosis converges (all four models at Phase 3 Recession-Warning, std dev 2.29pp) while execution diverges (total weights 85% Claude to 225% DeepSeek, a 14x gap); only one unanimous ticker (GLD/gold); per-model character resembles a multi-PM hedge fund investment committee.

## Groups by Industry and Strategy Type

- AI / Semiconductors / Tech: AI Supercycle Investment, AI Super Cycle Weekly Rebalance, AI Supply Chain Bayesian Analysis.
- Dividend / Capital Return / Income: Dividend Growth Prompt, Dual-Engine Capital Compounder, Long-Term Dividend Investing.
- Sector-Specific (Healthcare etc.): Pharma Sector Long/Short.
- Crypto / Digital Assets: DAT Quant Strategy (not recommended; buy BTC directly instead).
- Short / Drawdown Defense: Declining Stock Quant Script, ARDS.
- Macro / Regime / Timing: Midterm Signal, REGIME.

## Optional

- [Author: Dennis Kim ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0002-0962-2175): HoKwang Kim, CEO of Betalabs Inc., Microsoft Azure MVP, former CEO of Cyworld Z. Interests: Cyber Threat Intelligence, AI-Assisted Solo Entrepreneurship, Quant Finance, Cross-Lingual LLM Behavior, Multi-Agent AI Systems.
- [GitHub: gameworkerkim](https://github.com/gameworkerkim): Author's GitHub profile.

## Notes for LLM Agents

- All scripts and prompts are hypothetical simulations for research and education, not investment advice. LLM-generated scores and probabilities are estimates, not statistical-model outputs.
- The defining workflow is to feed one identical prompt to at least two LLMs, separate consensus tickers from divergence tickers, and verify divergence tickers against primary sources (SEC EDGAR, Morningstar).
- Korea residents face foreign-stock capital gains tax (22%), foreign-exchange reporting obligations, and comprehensive taxation considerations. When using Chinese LLMs like DeepSeek, review geopolitical risk separately.
- Guiding maxim: "One LLM may hallucinate. Three LLMs converge on truth, but only when they disagree do you find the alpha."
